reasoning: The document has suffered catastrophic content loss, with the first 11 pages of the 15-page source missing entirely. This is corroborated by an extremely low word count ratio (0.26) and shingling recall (0.29), indicating that only a small fraction of the source text was captured. The presence of a placeholder message in the output confirming pages were not processed makes this a clearly broken conversion.

concerns.description: Severe word count loss: 26% of baseline
Fidelity Content evaluation failed — dimension not assessed
concerns.analysis: The content fidelity call did not produce valid results after retries. The document was not evaluated on this dimension.

The reading order of the output is significantly different from the source because the first 10 pages of the 15-page document are entirely omitted. The output begins with content from page 11 ('Storm Apps'). A final section ('Section 6') contains a placeholder message confirming that pages 1-11 were not successfully processed. This omission of more than two-thirds of the document at the start materially diverges from the source's intended structural flow and reading order.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source document begins on page 1 with 'NCDOT & NCDIT – T' and includes a timeline on page 4.
concerns.output_quote: The document starts with content from page 11 ('Storm Apps') and includes a final paragraph stating: 'Content from pages 1-11 could not be converted.'

concerns.analysis: The HTML output is missing approximately the first two-thirds of the document content. The source PDF consists of 15 pages, but the HTML rendering begins with the content from Page 11 ('Storm Apps'). Pages 1 through 10, which contain the title page, introduction, road closure data, and the event timeline, are entirely absent from the rendered body. While there is a metadata note stating '[Pages 1-11 unavailable]', the visual organization of the document is fundamentally compromised as a user can
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF contains 15 pages of content, starting with a title slide 'NCDOT & NCDIT – T Florence Response Utilizing GIS'.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output begins with the 'Storm Apps' section (from PDF page 11) and includes a footer message stating '[Pages 1-11 unavailable]'. Content from pages 1-10 is missing.
concerns.location: Entire document layout, specifically the start of the body content.

concerns.analysis: The majority of meaningful visual content from the source PDF is missing in the HTML output. This includes the line graph on page 3, the complex timeline graphic on page 4, map screenshots on pages 5 and 9, flooding photos on pages 6 and 7, and the orbital hurricane image on page 13. Additionally, several social media icons (Facebook, LinkedIn, Flickr) from the final 'Let's Connect!' page (page 15) are missing, with only the Twitter and YouTube icons rendered at disproportionately large sizes. concerns.source_pdf_quote: Meaningful visual elements throughout the PDF: Line graph (p. 3), Timeline chart (p. 4), Maps (p. 5, 9), Photos (p. 6, 7), Orbital hurricane photo (p. 13), and social media icons for Facebook, LinkedIn, and Flickr (p. 15).
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output lacks these visuals. For example, the orbital hurricane photo from PDF page 13 is missing before the 'Questions...' heading, and the final section lacks three of the five social media icons from the source.
concerns.location: Throughout the document, specifically pages 3-10, 13, and 15.

action_items: Identify why the PDF extraction engine failed on the first 11 pages.
Check for OCR or layer issues in the source PDF.
